governing_requirement: Implement bounded retries only for typed transient errors, a Mode B circuit breaker, absolute deadline and declared resource enforcement, exact affected-scope blocking, and explicit no-silent-fallback behavior; outage, OOM, timeout, and incompatible-authority injection must block only dependent work where safe and never substitute an empty, wrong-owner, weaker-authority, or unqualified mask.
prerequisites: MF-P6-09.06 has no accepted producer service/API compatibility implementation.
MF-P6-11.03 has no accepted signed Mode B request/receipt/error exchange.
MF-P6-11.06 has no accepted durable bridge journal or atomic idempotency/retry state.
Main owns consumer retry, circuit-breaker, and DAG-impact execution; no pinned signed Main runtime evidence is available here.
verified_foundations: The frozen acquisition request binds an absolute created/deadline interval, declared runtime/queue/VRAM/RAM/output envelopes, maximum attempts from 1 through 10, typed-transient-only retry, and allow_silent_fallback fixed false.

The closed 37-code BRIDGE_ERROR_POLICY deterministically fixes category, retryability, impact scope, remediation action, retry-after requirement, and replacement-route requirement. Mutating its positive fixture to silent_fallback fails both schema and semantic policy validation.

The frozen lifecycle has request_retry_scheduled plus circuit_opened/circuit_closed transitions owned by Comfy_UI_Main, and the signed consumer-requirements fixture explicitly requires a circuit breaker.
Receipt validation recomputes deadline_met and rejects observed runtime, queue, VRAM, RAM, or output use beyond the request envelope after execution.

The producer runtime serializes heavy inference under one GPU lease, returns draft-only machine-candidate authority, rejects nonbinary/wrong-shape provider outputs, and does not substitute a provider when champion roles are absent.
Focused contract/runtime tests and Ruff pass.

commands: python -m ruff check src/maskfactory/validation.py src/maskfactory/serve/api.py src/maskfactory/serve/comfy_export.py tests/test_mask_bridge_contracts_v1.py tests/test_serve_api.py
direct expired-deadline, minimum-resource-envelope, error-policy, implementation-search, and service-refusal probes

direct_probes.gap: validate_mask_acquisition_request checks created-before-deadline ordering but accepts no current/decision time and therefore does not reject a currently expired request.

direct_probes.interpretation: The request is structurally valid, but no admission path compares the envelope with current queue/model/device requirements before work begins. Receipt checks are retrospective only.
The wire policy forbids silent fallback, but the raw HTTP and node paths do not emit or consume this typed error record.

direct_probes.observed: MFPredictMasks performs one urllib request with a fixed 120-second transport timeout and collapses OSError, ValueError, and URLError into ComfyPackageError.
FastAPI catches serving-domain errors and emits generic HTTP 503 detail. Prior live zero-champion probes returned champion prediction provider is not configured without provider substitution.
direct_probes.gap: The response is not a signed mask_bridge_error and carries no deterministic retry/scope/remediation record.

direct_probes.observed: Bridge-specific circuit/retry/deadline/resource terms outside validation/contracts are absent; unrelated DAZ, provider, VLM, filesystem, and legacy pipeline retry utilities do not implement the frozen bridge decision matrix.
direct_probes.gap: No persistent breaker state, threshold/window/half-open transition, scoped controller block, or signed decision evidence exists.

confirmed_gaps: No contract-native adapter or producer endpoint consumes the signed request deadline, retry policy, or resource envelope before admission/execution.

No bounded retry engine accepts only validated typed transient errors, accounts attempts atomically with the idempotency/journal record, honors retry-after/deadline, or forbids retry of semantic/authority failures at runtime.
No circuit-breaker store, release/route key, failure threshold, observation window, cooldown, half-open probe, or signed open/close evidence is implemented.
No absolute deadline covers queue, lease, execution, receipt validation, and all retries; the node's fixed 120-second socket timeout is not the signed request deadline.
No producer admission controller proves the selected model/route can fit the declared GPU/RAM/output envelope, and no typed resource refusal/OOM record is returned over the live API.
No Main-owned scoped DAG blocker consumes impact_scope/completion-profile effects and proves unrelated passes continue while exact dependent passes remain blocked.
The live service and thin node exchange generic 503/ComfyPackageError text rather than a validated signed mask_bridge_error record.

No outage, OOM, timeout, incompatible-authority, circuit-open, deadline-expiry, or resource-refusal end-to-end fault matrix proves that empty, wrong-owner, weaker-authority, stale, or unqualified masks are never substituted.
No signed journal/receipt evidence binds retry decisions, circuit state, resource observations, scoped blocking, and final abstain/fail outcomes across restart/concurrency.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement additive producer failure-control admission and conformance: validate current deadlines and resource feasibility, return typed signed bridge errors, enforce producer-owned no-fallback/refusal behavior, and validate signed Main retry/circuit/scoped-block evidence without implementing or impersonating Main's DAG controller.
implementation_packet.path_state: All five allowed paths are absent and clean. Candidate paths remain read-only. The scope is disjoint from frozen v1 contracts, preserved MF-P6-08.05 dirty paths, and prepared MF-P6-09.06/11.03/11.06 paths.
validator_commands: python -m pytest -q tests/test_bridge_failure_control.py tests/test_mask_bridge_contracts_v1.py tests/test_serve_api.py -k 'retry or circuit or deadline or resource or fallback or error or authority'
python -m ruff check src/maskfactory/bridge/failure_control.py tools/check_bridge_failure_control.py tests/test_bridge_failure_control.py
acceptance_criteria: At admission and every later boundary, compare trusted current time with the signed absolute deadline; expired work fails before provider invocation and no retry may exceed the remaining deadline.

Compare the signed queue/runtime/VRAM/RAM/output envelope with current route requirements and observations; refuse infeasible work with the exact typed error and never silently choose CPU, a smaller/unqualified model, another owner, or weaker authority unless an explicit signed compatible route permits it.
Map producer exceptions to a validated signed mask_bridge_error whose code fixes retryability, impact, remediation, and state transition; generic string/HTTP status cannot control retries.

Persist attempt budgets and retry decisions atomically with idempotency/journal state. Retry only validated typed transient errors with bounded backoff/retry-after; authority, semantic, hash, transform, ownership, schema, and idempotency failures are terminal until a new valid request/state change.

Validate signed Main circuit evidence keyed to exact adopted release/capability/route, including threshold/window/cooldown/half-open probe and legal open/close events; MaskFactory does not sign or execute Main's circuit state.
Validate a signed Main scoped-impact decision that blocks only exact dependent passes/resources where safe, preserves unrelated work, and contains no empty/wrong/weaker/unqualified fallback artifact.

Fault tests cover service outage, socket timeout, queue/runtime deadline, OOM/resource refusal, circuit open/half-open, incompatible authority, stale/revoked certificate, unknown error, and restart/concurrency. Each proves exact typed disposition and no silent mask substitution.

Frozen v1 contracts, preserved dirty paths, worker records, and unrelated service/runtime state remain byte-identical. Any missing v1 semantic requires an additive internal evidence schema or negotiated future wire version, never mutation of frozen v1.

claim_boundary: This audit credits the strict frozen request/error/event contracts, closed error policy, receipt-side deadline/resource validation, GPU serialization/lease, draft authority, output-shape enforcement, and no-provider-substitution behavior only. It does not implement or prove contract-native typed errors, bounded retries, a circuit breaker, admission-time deadline/resource enforcement, scoped Main DAG blocking, restart-safe attempt state, or the required end-to-end fault matrix, and it does not complete MF-P6-11.07.
